edit_diagnosis: The reward schema is dominated by a repeatable event bonus for contact, leading to reward hacking (high reward but poor task progress). Converting r_contact to a one‑time event will prevent repeated exploitation and incentivize a single successful landing.

The one-time contact edit successfully reduced reward hacking, but the dense shaping components remain too harsh/unaligned, resulting in very low episode rewards and task failure. The reward structure now needs a structural overhaul to guide the agent toward a successful landing, not another targeted hack fix.
